bullets.text: Net income rose 31% to a record $1.65 billion ($7.63 per share) for 2004; industry estimated to produce perhaps its lowest combined ratio in history, with a likely three-peat in 2005.
Net premiums written grew approximately $1.5 billion, or 12%; industry-wide personal auto NPW grew an estimated 3.7%, down sharply from the prior three-year average of 8%.

Dutch auction repurchased 16.9 million shares at $88 for $1.5 billion plus 1.7 million open-market shares; ended 2004 with 200.4 million shares and $13.1 billion invested assets; debt-to-capital at 20%.
Best-ever claim file quality achieved in 2004; concierge service expanded to 20 centers, with disciplined planning positioning aggressive expansion through 2005–2006.
Drive Insurance from Progressive launched for independent agents; two-brand strategy (Drive and Progressive Direct) creates distinct identities for agency and direct channels.
New billing system and improved claims management platform deployed; 2005 roadmap includes improved customer management systems and new data center infrastructure.
Retention improvement fell short of expectations in 2004; management elevated it to a key company initiative, committing to extraordinary customer experience as the only acceptable standard.
Four hurricanes tested operations; Progressive people managed claims with assistance from across the company and received strong customer praise during the response.
Progressive Direct was the fastest-growing top-15 insurer at 16.5%; Agency Business ranked fifth at 9.5%; scale, IT, pricing segmentation, and claims cited as key consolidation drivers.
New Jersey market entry anticipated in 2005 pending new ratemaking regulations; Progressive actively handling claims there as a servicing carrier for the Commercial Auto Insurance Plan.
